description: Figure 6 presents multiscale microstructural characterization of the M2-IA sample (Fe-8Mn-3Al-1Si-0.2C steel intercritically annealed at 700°C for 1 h). Panels (a1–a4) display scanning electron microscopy (SEM) images at progressively higher magnifications (scale bars: 200 μm, 20 μm, 5 μm, and 3 μm), revealing a three-phase microstructure comprising coarse band-type δ-ferrite, polygonal α-ferrite, and fine martensite-austenite (α′+γ) constituents. Yellow arrows explicitly label these phases across the subfigures, illustrating the morphological distribution from macroscopic banding to fine submicron structures. Panel (b) provides an electron backscatter diffraction (EBSD) phase map (5 μm scale bar) where the orange-colored phase represents austenite (γ), confirming its spatial distribution within the ferritic matrix. Together, these images demonstrate the microstructural heterogeneity of the Si-containing steel, notably the persistence of δ-ferrite bands and the morphology of retained austenite, which collectively govern the austenite stability and mechanical performance compared to the Si-free variant.
